validated_commands.benchmark: python3 -m pytest tests/test_processing.py tests/test_filters.py -k 'benchmark and block_processor' --override-ini='addopts=' -p no:codspeed --benchmark-warmup=on --benchmark-min-rounds=50 -q
validated_commands.benchmark_meter: python3 -m pytest tests/test_processing.py -k 'benchmark and meter' --override-ini='addopts=' -p no:codspeed --benchmark-warmup=on --benchmark-min-rounds=50 -q
validated_commands.install: pip install -e . --break-system-packages && pip install pytest pytest-benchmark --break-system-packages
validated_commands.note: uv not available in CI runner; use pip install fallback above

optimization_backlog.desc: Replace RunningSum property overhead with plain float for _calc_momentary and _calc_short_term. PR submitted 2026-05-01, branch perf-assist/flat-float-window-accumulators.
optimization_backlog.notes: Eliminates 4-6 property getter/setter accesses per block (equality checks, mean invalidation).

optimization_backlog.desc: lk_log10() uses np.log10 for scalars; math.log10 is ~1.4x faster for scalar path. ~1% benchmark improvement only.
optimization_backlog.impact: ~1% per benchmark mode (too small for CodSpeed, probably)

optimization_backlog.desc: Maintainer is open to exploring vendoring/trimming _upfirdn_apply.pyx on a SEPARATE branch (not main). Requires Cython. High complexity but potentially significant impact (~55% CPU).
optimization_backlog.impact: Up to 55% of Meter.write time is TruePeak FIR
optimization_backlog.feasibility: low-medium (complex, maintainer interested in separate branch exploration)
optimization_backlog.notes: Maintainer note 2026-04-30: 'It may be worth re-addressing with your help, but I wouldn't want to do that in the main branch.'

optimization_backlog.desc: _calc_gating does O(block_index) numpy work per block when rel_threshold changes; O(n^2) total.

maintainer_instructions: Remove reset() optimization from backlog - moved to teardown in benchmarks (PR #99, 2026-04-29)
Open to exploring _upfirdn_apply.pyx vendoring on separate branch (not main) — mentioned 2026-04-30 in issue #71 comment
perf_notes: CodSpeed 'does not alter performance' = overall improvement below 10% threshold. Individual benchmarks accessible via CodSpeed link.
Only algorithmic changes or large constant-factor changes exceed 10% CodSpeed threshold reliably.
scipy _apply (upfirdn) ACCUMULATES into output buffer (out[i] += contribution), not overwrites. out[...]=0 zeroing in _UpFIRDn.apply_filter is NECESSARY.
numpy memory pool recycles same-size allocations very efficiently: np.empty(3MB) then free is ~0 overhead. Pre-allocated buffers do not help for same-size allocations.
TruePeakProcessor.process bottleneck: 55.6% CPU at 48kHz. All of it is in cython _apply FIR convolution. No wrapper-level wins possible.
sosfilt bottleneck: 22.98% CPU. Input copy (40KB x 46 calls/round) = ~1.6% max improvement, below threshold.
Benchmark call counts per round (48kHz, gate=19200, block=1024): 46 sosfilt calls, 2 TruePeakProcessor.process calls
n_out = gate_size * upsample_factor for all valid gate sizes (verified for up=4 and up=2).
np.sum(a*b) vs np.dot(a,b): for 2-element arrays, np.dot is 4.2x faster - MERGED PR #98
np.mean on 3-30 element slices: 4.8-5.1x slower than Python float running sum - MERGED PR #100
RunningSum property overhead: equality check setter + mean invalidation adds ~6 property accesses per _calc_momentary call. Flat float eliminates this - SUBMITTED PR (2026-05-01)
np.square(full_block) reuse (PR #95): +7% in momentary/short_term/lra per CodSpeed - MERGED
pytest-codspeed teardown (reset()) is NOT included in benchmark timing (moved to teardown fixture in PR #99)
CodSpeed simulation mode counts CPU instructions - Python dispatch overhead matters significantly
pip install fallback: pip install -e . --break-system-packages (uv not available in runner)

infrastructure_notes.codspeed_threshold: CodSpeed 'does not alter performance' = below 10% overall threshold. Individual benchmarks accessible via CodSpeed link.
infrastructure_notes.benchmark_coverage: All 4 block_processor modes: integrated, momentary, short_term, lra. Plus meter (4 sample rates). NEW: TruePeakProcessor standalone (48kHz 4x, 88.2kHz 2x).
infrastructure_notes.what_shows_in_codspeed: Algorithmic changes (O(n^2) -> O(n)) or dispatch elimination for frequently-called operations. Sub-2% local improvements likely invisible in CodSpeed.
